subject: The Indiana Firewall
body:
This year's elections are about more than just 2018. They're about more than our six Republican candidates for the U.S. Senate.... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6b0afb2924&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_The Indiana Firewall_** This year's elections are about more than just 2018. They're about more than our six Republican candidates for the U.S. Senate. And they're even about more than just Democrat Joe Donnelly.

This year's elections are about the heart and soul of America. They're about control of the United States Senate -- and, likewise, they're about the future of America. As a country, are we going to continue supporting **President Donald Trump** and **Vice President Mike Pence** and put America first, or are we going to look back on the failed, America-last agenda of Obama, Schumer and Pelosi?

At Congress of Counties earlier this month, **Senator Todd Young** \-- our soon-to-be senior senator -- put it best. He said, "We're going to have an Indiana firewall in 2018!" This firewall will protect our Republican majority in the United States Senate -- with one less Democrat (see ya, Democrat Donnelly), and one more Republican fighting for us in Washington.

But we can only do that by making sure we come together in May, and commit to support our Republican U.S. Senate nominee following the conclusion of our primary.

Until then, love your candidate. Fight for them, make phone calls, knock on doors -- work tirelessly to help him win. But then come May 9, it's time to come together as Republicans, and bring that same passion to support our nominee. The future of America depends on it.

Indiana is going to be the firewall protecting the U.S. Senate in 2018! But first, we must ensure we vote to send Todd Young a Republican reinforcement for the Senate in November.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Picture:[Watch Chairman Kyle Hupfer's impassioned speech](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d08afb8e59&e=1acd9bb3d6) at Congress of Counties, as he discusses our six U.S. Senate candidates -- and the need to come together after the primary to send one of them to Washington. _
---
| |
---

| Today more than 200,000 service men and women leave the military each year, and more than half of them face a period of unemployment before finding their next job. That's while Indiana has about 85,000 unfilled open positions -- waiting for someone with the skills and work ethic of a veteran to fill them.

Earlier today, **Governor Eric Holcomb** and **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch** launched a new program focused on helping those veterans find a new home and a bright future in Indiana.

Indiana's latest Next Level initiative -- Next Level Veterans -- is a statewide program that's bringing public and private organizations together to recruit, employ and connect discharging military personnel in Indiana, as well as provide special support for our current Hoosier veterans.

If you're a veteran, or know a veteran who may benefit from this program, make sure to check out [www.in.gov/veterans](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a636db559b&e=1acd9bb3d6) to learn more!

Congratulations to Hoosier **Alex Azar** \-- the former president of Indiana-based Lilly USA -- on [his](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b180ee696d&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ confirmation](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=aa4dfc0d44&e=1acd9bb3d6) as the new U.S. Secretary for Health and Human Services!

We look forward to watching his work with President Donald Trump to help fix our federal health care system!

| [Read More](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c747ad318f&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Read More")

Earlier today, major Hoosier employer FedEx announced that it is [investing $1.5 billion into its Indiana hub](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=39add051bd&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- in addition to increasing wages, bonuses and pension funding for its Hoosier workforce -- as a direct result of federal tax reform.

Thanks to our entire Republican congressional delegation for voting for tax reform -- and no thanks to Democrat Joe Donnelly, who voted "no" on putting more money in Hoosier pockets!
| [Read More](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=05ea006cac&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Read More")

Congratulations to Indiana's newest state senator, **Brian Buchanan** ,[ who was elected at last night's district caucus](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=371c7db635&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Former State Senator Brandt Hershman served this district honorably over his distinguished career in the Indiana Senate -- helping Indiana rise to become the fiscal envy of the nation. We know that State Senator-elect Buchanan will work with that same passion to serve Hoosiers throughout the district!

1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f405fe53396efdefec16cb4ab693e18588790e55c2c9912219db9daeddf3d33244c2c53ddc880f20fc3a210e79060a79ac
'We want peace and prosperity' says Trump as he storms Davos with offers of better trade deals and pleas for big business to invest in America

- Daily Mail

In Switzerland, President Donald J. Trump has been advocating for America, meeting with key American allies, and making a pitch for better trade deals. “We want great prosperity and we want great peace,” the President told reporters.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f4a55a67724737182018ab58a1eab110fbd87d7e09da046405f298772bdfd0afbc10542bffe1df25d9c5cd80c5d161b574

U.S. heads for 3% growth trifecta on spending, investment punch

- Bloomberg News

The U.S. economy continues to accelerate, with the “longest stretch of 3 percent-or-better growth since 2005.” American consumer confidence, business investment, and a rebound in housing all led to strong numbers. “Tax cuts championed by President Donald Trump have fueled expectations of an extended boom in capital spending and buoyed household confidence.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f4cc2a4371d8898537f1aaaa250e6cecb55d47be547920def0ecf776f6deb81fc48e01e6ed80e7fd0a24a18f51a49a75b6
U.S. imposes more North Korea sanctions; urges China, Russia expulsions

- Reuters

This week the United States announced new sanctions on North Korea aimed at stopping the country’s nuclear weapons development. The sanctions also urged China and Russia to expel North Koreans who raise funds for these programs.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f436e7fcf02a0f29f305731e1777205f8ba0f9a1249b080a4e9e1bf398309079a370027daaa952d2f63302e33665d5beb2

Melania Trump pays respect to Holocaust victims in recognition of Remembrance Day

- The Daily Caller

First Lady Melania Trump visited the United States Holocaust Memorial Museum on Thursday to pay respects ahead of today’s International Holocaust Remembrance Day. “My thoughts and prayers are with the people whose lives and families were broken by the horrors of the Holocaust. Yet it is also through our shared humanity that we come together now in commemoration, strength, and love,” the First Lady said.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f46aaceef44dcc79072f7eb6103ba2e6dce56a1dc71bb6319c56316a1588cf2359c7e58a0979654ae42414a579b52dac68
Home Depot hourly employees to receive up to $1,000 bonus due to tax reform

- CNBC

Home Depot joined a growing list of corporations “using new tax benefits to invest in their workers” by offering bonuses to employees of up to $1,000. As the U.S. labor market tightens, bonus payments and wage increases can “help incentivize current employees to stick around.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f4c531bdf9d58351edb323ca1a6d773f5cb0261586ca3045667f9df2a472ad3eafe7b2372d112f4738317550a43152d872
Trump comments lift dollar, Dow hits record high

- Financial Times

On Thursday afternoon, the U.S. dollar “staged a comeback” on a “show of support” from President Trump, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at a new record high.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=31c1c1b4817494f478a29a84926c79977d40e37754245c2f1abfca43515ed88737aa802bc285537b97aa4272132d0e5cb920beb2045651ba
Alex Azar confirmed by Senate as new head of Health and Human Services

- The Washington Post

On Wednesday, the Senate voted to confirm Alex Azar as the next Secretary of Health and Human Services. The vote marked the “third time that Azar has won Senate confirmation,” given his previous administration experience serving at the Department of Health and Human Services.

name: DNC HQ
subject: Let's be clear about this, Derek
body:
In just a few days, we face our first end-of-month fundraising deadline of 2018.
---
Let me be clear about something, Derek:

Organizing in every single community across the country is the only way we will win this year. It's the only way we can talk to voters about what matters to them and elect Democrats from city halls to Congress. But here's the thing -- organizing is expensive, and if we don't invest now, we won't be in a position to win come November.

In a few days, we face our first fundraising deadline of 2018. Each contribution we receive before that deadline will show that Democrats are ready to take back Congress this year.

**[Chip in $3 before our January deadline to send a clear message to Trump and the GOP that we're ready to win back a majority in Congress this November. The only way we will do that is if we work together.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522214/4067b0a/4bd6e4209/7e503a6a/3605358085/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/january-deadline-2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+Chip+in+3+before+our+January+deadline+to&utm_campaign=em_20180127_js_nd&source=em_20180127_js_nd&refcode=em_20180127_js_nd")**

Last year we showed what is possible when we come together and organize. Now it's time to take our success nationwide.

Thanks for being a part of it.

Joe

Joe Smolskis
Chief Financial Officer
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522214/4067b0a/4bd6e4209/7e503a76/3605358085/VEsP/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180127_js_nd&source=em_20180127_js_nd&refcode=em_20180127_js_nd"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522214/4067b0a/4bd6e4209/7e503a75/3605358085/VEsHBA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180127_js_nd&source=em_20180127_js_nd&refcode=em_20180127_js_nd"), or just reply to this email! I'm fighting my hardest to re-elect our ELEVEN Democratic Senate women in 2018 and grow our ranks. Are you with me?

Team,

Last year, I was one of two Democratic Senate candidates who flipped GOP seats -- and both of us were women.

Now, two women are once again running for Senate in the two states that represent our best pick up opportunities in 2018.

If we're going to win back the Senate, Jacky Rosen in Nevada and Kyrsten Sinema in Arizona need our support to launch strong campaigns and flip these seats blue.

[**Candidates like Jacky and Kyrsten are counting on you, team. Pitch in $1 right now to the Women 's Senate Network to elect more women who can flip the Senate.**](http://action.dscc. When women lead in the Senate, we bring unique perspectives to the table that help us better advocate for the people we serve.

My fellow Democratic Senate women and I are pushing forward on the issues that make a difference in women's lives, from providing every woman with paid maternity leave to guaranteeing equal pay for equal work.

Now ELEVEN of my female Democratic Senate colleagues are up for re-election next year -- and we can't afford to lose a single one. That's why I'm fighting my hardest to make sure that we send each one of them back to Washington in 2018 -- and elect more women to join us.

| | Peter -- we've been telling you all along: Mitch McConnell's tax plan was built to help his rich allies. And his deep-pocketed donors like the Koch brothers are already reaping the benefits. They're reportedly set to earn over a billion dollars more from McConnell's tax plan this year alone. This is how emboldened the Kochs have become. Within two weeks of the McConnell tax bill passing, they donated tens of thousands of dollars to the RNC and nearly $500,000 to Paul Ryan. In a rare moment of truth, they even said that thanks to the McConnell tax bill, they were getting a great return on their political investments. **Time and again, we 've asked you to chip into to Joe's campaign and stand with a candidate who shares our values.
